title: BOM Decomposition — NAICS 531120 Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings

sections.title: B.1 — L4 Subsystems
sections.content:
The behind-the-meter containerized BESS plant decomposes into 5 L4 subsystems. The Control/Interface subsystem (EMS) is critical here — without it, the asset has no revenue stream.

| L4 # | Subsystem | Function | Pattern role |
|---|---|---|---|
| L4-1 | Battery Storage Subsystem | Store and release DC electrical energy | Primary functional core |
| L4-2 | Power Conversion Subsystem (PCS) | Convert DC↔AC, dispatch to grid/load | Primary functional core |
| L4-3 | MV Grid Coupling Subsystem | Step up to MV, switch, protect, meter | Primary functional core |
| L4-4 | Control / EMS Subsystem | Optimize charge/discharge, comms, cybersecurity | Control/interface |
| L4-5 | Civil & Site Subsystem | Foundation, trenching, fencing, earthing | Containment/support |

**No standalone "packaging" subsystem** — the ISO container envelope is part of L4-1 (it contains the batteries and is sold as a unit).

### L5 → L4 alternatives

**L5 [BESS Plant] → L4 [Subsystem decomposition]**
This decomposition is **architecturally standard** for grid-connected BESS. There are no "alternative subsystem groupings" — every commercial BESS contains these five.

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5-subsystem decomposition (Battery / PCS / MV / EMS / Civil) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | Industry standard per IEC 62933, EN 50549 |

`single-source: true` (architectural convention).

---

sections.title: L4-1 — Battery Storage Subsystem (containerized)
sections.content:
```
L4-1 Battery Storage Subsystem
 ├─ L3-1.1 Battery rack module
 ├─ L3-1.2 Battery management system (BMS)
 ├─ L3-1.3 Thermal management module
 ├─ L3-1.4 Fire detection & suppression module
 └─ L3-1.5 Container envelope
```

### L4-1 → L3 [Battery storage architecture]

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Containerized rack-based (modular shelves, separate BMS/thermal/fire) | ~92% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [IDTechEx BESS thermal report](https://www.idtechex.com/en/research-report/thermal-management-fire-and-explosion-protection-for-bess/1127) |
| All-in-one cabinet (skid, integrated <500 kWh) | ~8% | → Stable | MED | [Mordor Intelligence BESS](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/global-battery-cell-market) |

### L3-1.1 Battery rack module → L2

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LFP prismatic rack (280–314 Ah) | ~85–90% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/global-battery-cell-market) |
| Pouch cell module rack | ~5–10% | → Stable | MED | [Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/global-battery-cell-market) |
| Cylindrical (4680/21700) rack | ~5% | → Stable | MED | [CIC energiGUNE](https://cicenergigune.com/en/blog/format-battery-cell-manufacturers-prismatic-cylindrical-pouch) |

### L2 LFP prismatic rack → L1 [Parts]

`Parts: cells, busbars, rack frame, cell-level fuses, voltage/temperature sense wiring.`

**Alternatives at L2→L1 (cell chemistry):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| LFP (LiFePO₄) | ~95% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [Wood Mackenzie](https://www.woodmac.com/press-releases/lfp-to-overtake-nmc-as-dominant-stationary-storage-chemistry-by-2030/) |
| NMC | ~3–5% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | [Energy-Storage.News / BNEF](https://www.energy-storage.news/lfp-to-dominate-3twh-global-lithium-ion-battery-market-by-2030/) |
| Na-ion | <1% | ↑ Emerging | MED | [BNEF](https://about.bnef.com/insights/clean-energy/global-energy-storage-boom-three-things-to-know/) |
| LTO (niche) | <0.5% | → Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |
| NCA | <1% | ↓ Declining | MED | analyst estimate |

### L1 → L0 [Cell raw materials, LFP path]

| L1 part | L0 raw material | Alt. share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cathode active material | LFP powder (LiFePO₄) | 100% (this branch) | → Stable | HIGH | [USGS Mineral Commodity Summary 2025](https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center/lithium-statistics-and-information) |
| Anode active material | Graphite (synthetic vs natural) | Synthetic 65% / Natural 35% | → Stable | HIGH | [Benchmark Mineral Intelligence](https://source.benchmarkminerals.com/) |
| Electrolyte | LiPF₆ in EC/DMC carbonate | ~100% (Li-ion std.) | → Stable | HIGH | [USGS](https://www.usgs.gov/centers/national-minerals-information-center) |
| Separator | Ceramic-coated polyolefin (PE/PP) | ~95% | → Stable | HIGH | industry standard |
| Cathode foil | Aluminum foil | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source |
| Anode foil | Copper foil | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source |
| Cell case | Prismatic Al case | 100% (LFP path) | → Stable | HIGH | single-source |
| Busbar | Cu OR Al busbar | Cu 70% / Al 30% | → Stable | MED | analyst estimate |

`single-source: true` for electrolyte, separator (chemistry-locked).

### L3-1.2 BMS → L2 → L1 → L0

`L2 alternatives: 3-tier BMS (BMU + BCMS + BAMS) vs flat 2-tier vs distributed.`

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 3-tier hierarchical (BMU/BCMS/BAMS) | ~75% | → Stable | MED | [Sungrow BMS architecture](https://en.sungrowpower.com/) |
| 2-tier flat | ~20% | ↓ Declining | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Distributed cell-controller | ~5% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L1 parts:** MCU/SoC PCBA, voltage isolators, current shunts, balancing FETs, NTC thermistors, isolated CAN/RS-485 transceivers.
**L0 raw materials:** Silicon (wafer), FR4 epoxy laminate, copper traces, tin/SAC305 solder, encapsulants.

| L1 → L0 | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Si die for MCU | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source |
| FR4 PCB substrate | ~95% (Halogen-free FR4) | → Stable | MED | analyst estimate |
| SAC305 solder (lead-free) | ~95% (RoHS) | → Stable | HIGH | EU RoHS Directive |
| Tin-lead solder (legacy) | ~5% | ↓ Declining | MED | analyst estimate |

### L3-1.3 Thermal management → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Liquid-cooled (glycol-water cold plates) | ~70–80% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [IDTechEx](https://www.idtechex.com/en/research-report/thermal-management-fire-and-explosion-protection-for-bess/1127) |
| Air-cooled (forced HVAC) | ~20–25% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | [IDTechEx](https://www.idtechex.com/en/research-article/safer-battery-storage-thermal-management-and-fire-protection-trends/33916) |
| Dielectric immersion | <2% | ↑ Emerging | MED | [IDTechEx](https://www.idtechex.com/en/research-report/thermal-management-fire-and-explosion-protection-for-bess/1127) |

**L2 (liquid path):** chiller skid + manifold + cold plates.
**L1:** scroll compressor, brazed-plate evaporator, circulation pump, expansion valve, pipework.
**L0:** Aluminum (cold plates ~95% / Cu ~5%), R-454B refrigerant (post-R-410A phase-out, 70%) / R-1234yf (15%) / propylene-glycol+water for low-side loop (15%), brazed Al alloy (4045/3003), copper tube.

| L1→L0 alt: refrigerant | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| R-454B (low-GWP) | ~70% (new EU systems) | ↑ Growing | MED | [EU F-Gas Reg. 2024/573](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/573/oj) |
| R-410A | ~15% (legacy) | ↓ Declining | HIGH | [EU F-Gas Reg.](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/573/oj) |
| R-1234yf | ~10% | ↑ Growing | MED | analyst estimate |
| Propylene-glycol (water side) | ~5% (secondary loop) | → Stable | HIGH | industry standard |

### L3-1.4 Fire suppression → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Aerosol (Stat-X / FirePro) | ~45% | ↑ Growing | LOW | analyst estimate |
| FK-5-1-12 / NOVEC 1230 | ~25% | ↓ Declining (3M EOL 2025) | MED | [Fike on 3M discontinuation](https://www.fike.com/knowledge-center/3m-novec-1230-discontinued-what-now/) |
| Water mist (UL 9540A) | ~20% | ↑ Growing | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Inert gas (N₂/Ar/IG-541) | ~10% | → Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L2:** detection panel + suppression cylinder + nozzles + venting damper.
**L1:** smoke/CO/H₂/HF sensors, agent cylinder, pressure switch, control panel.
**L0:** suppression agent (above), steel cylinder (DIN-EN 1442), copper tubing.

### L3-1.5 Container envelope → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 20ft HC ISO (3–5 MWh, dense LFP) | ~35% | ↑ Growing | MED | [Acebattery](https://www.acebattery.com/blogs/bess-container-sizes-how-to-choose-the-right-capacity) |
| 20ft ISO (≤1 MWh) | ~45% | → Stable | MED | [MarketsandMarkets](https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/containerized-battery-energy-storage-system-bess-market-81525510.html) |
| 40ft ISO | ~15% | ↓ Declining | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Custom skid (<500 kWh) | ~5% | → Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L2:** corrugated steel side panels + roof + door + insulation lining.
**L1:** Corten/galvanized steel panels, mineral-wool insulation batts, gasket seals, IP54 vents.
**L0:** Corten weathering steel (~70%) / hot-dip galvanized steel (~30%), rock-wool insulation, EPDM rubber gaskets, PU paint.

---

sections.title: L4-2 — Power Conversion Subsystem
sections.content:
```
L4-2 PCS
 ├─ L3-2.1 Inverter unit (PCS)
 ├─ L3-2.2 DC-side BoS
 └─ L3-2.3 AC-side filtering
```

### L4-2 → L3 [PCS architecture]

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| String PCS (100–430 kW units, parallel) | ~55–60% (C&I) | ↑ Growing | MED | [CLOU Global](https://clouglobal.com/string-pcs-vs-centralized-pcs-maximizing-reliability-and-availability-in-large-scale-energy-storage/) |
| Central PCS (>1 MW single block) | ~35–40% | ↓ Declining | MED | [KACO whitepaper](https://kaco-newenergy.com/fileadmin/downloads/documents/KNE-PAP-String-Inverters-for-Energy-Storage.pdf) |
| Modular DC-block (PowerTitan-style) | ~5–10% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | analyst estimate |

### L3-2.1 PCS unit → L2 → L1 → L0

**L2 power module + DC link.**

**Vendor share within L2 (DE/EU C&I, informational only — vendors are not BOM alternatives):**

| Vendor (informational) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SMA Solar | ~20–25% | → Stable | MED | [pv-magazine / WoodMac](https://www.pv-magazine.com/2026/01/05/huawei-sungrow-lead-wood-mackenzies-solar-inverter-market-ranking/) |
| Sungrow | ~17% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [WoodMac](https://www.woodmac.com/press-releases/2024-press-releases/global-pv-inverter-shipments-grew-by-56-in-2023-to-536-gwac/) |
| Power Electronics | ~5–8% | ↑ Growing | MED | [MarketsandMarkets](https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/ResearchInsight/inverter-market.asp) |
| Ingeteam | ~5% | → Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Hitachi Energy | ~3–5% | ↑ Growing | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Others (KACO, Schneider, Gamesa-Electric) | ~30% | mixed | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L1 parts:** IGBT modules (or SiC MOSFETs), DC-link film capacitors, AC inductors, gate drivers, heatsink, cooling fan.

**L1→L0 alternatives (power switch semiconductor):**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Si IGBT | ~85% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | [Yole Power Semi 2024](https://www.yolegroup.com/) |
| SiC MOSFET | ~12% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [Yole Power Semi 2024](https://www.yolegroup.com/) |
| GaN HEMT (low-power aux) | <3% | ↑ Emerging | MED | analyst estimate |

**L0 raw materials:** Si wafer, SiC wafer (4H-SiC), Cu (busbars), Al (heatsink), grain-oriented electrical steel (chokes), polypropylene film (DC caps).

### L3-2.2 DC-side BoS → L2 → L1 → L0

`single-source: true` for L2 (DC combiner architecture is standardized).
**L1 parts:** DC fuses (gG, NH-style), DC contactors, busbars, surge arresters.
**L0 alt: contactor:** EV-grade sealed contactor 60% / Open-frame 40% (analyst estimate, MED). **L0 raw:** Cu (busbars 100%, single-source HIGH), silver/W contact tips (single-source).

### L3-2.3 AC-side filtering → L2 → L1 → L0

`L2: LCL filter + sine filter cabinet.` `single-source: true` (architecture is standard).
**L1:** line reactors (Fe-core), filter capacitors (PP film), output contactor.
**L0:** Grain-oriented electrical steel (M3-M6), Cu wire, polypropylene film capacitor dielectric, epoxy potting.

---

sections.title: L4-3 — MV Grid Coupling Subsystem
sections.content:
```
L4-3 MV Grid Coupling
 ├─ L3-3.1 MV transformer
 ├─ L3-3.2 MV switchgear
 ├─ L3-3.3 Protection / NA-protection
 └─ L3-3.4 Metering & SCADA gateway
```

### L4-3 → L3 [MV architecture]

`single-source: true`. Every VDE-AR-N 4110 plant has these four blocks (transformer + switchgear + protection + metering).

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Standard 4-block MV interface (transformer + RMU + relay + metering) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | [VDE-AR-N 4110](https://www.vde.com/en/fnn/topics/technical-connection-rules/tcr-for-medium-voltage) |

### L3-3.1 MV transformer → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alt. (insulation medium) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mineral-oil filled | ~70–75% | ↓ Declining | MED | [Mordor Intelligence — EU distribution transformer](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/europe-distribution-transformer-market) |
| Dry-type cast resin (urban/indoor) | ~15–20% | → Stable | MED | [Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/europe-distribution-transformer-market) |
| Ester-filled (natural / synthetic) | ~10–15% | ↑ Growing | MED | [Business Research Insights](https://www.businessresearchinsights.com/market-reports/ester-transformer-oils-market-108881) |

**L2:** tank + magnetic core + HV/LV windings + bushings + tap-changer.
**L1:** core laminations, HV/LV copper windings, porcelain/silicone bushings, OLTC (optional), Buchholz relay.
**L0:** Grain-oriented electrical steel (~100% for cores, single-source HIGH), Cu winding (~95%) / Al winding (~5%), mineral oil OR natural ester OR cast-resin epoxy (per L2 split above), porcelain or silicone-rubber for bushings.

### L3-3.2 MV switchgear → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alt. (insulation medium, EU C&I post-2026) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SF6-free dry-air + vacuum | ~40% (→ ~95% post-Jan 2026) | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [Schneider/E.ON SF6-free deployment](https://switchgearcontent.com/2025/09/04/4242/schneider-electric-partners-with-e-on-to-deploy-sf6-free-switchgear-across-europe/) |
| SF6 (legacy) | ~50% (→ 0% by 2026 ban ≤24kV) | ↓ Declining | HIGH | [Schneider on EU F-Gas](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2025/07/18/is-your-data-center-ready-for-the-ban-on-sf6-mv-switchgear-in-the-eu/) |
| Solid-insulated (SIS) | ~10% | → Stable | MED | [DataIntelo](https://dataintelo.com/report/global-gas-insulated-switchgear-sf6-free-market) |

**L2:** cubicle + circuit breaker + busbar compartment + earthing switch.
**L1:** vacuum interrupter bottle, Cu busbar, epoxy spacer/insulator, SF6-free puffer (or vacuum + dry-air mix), aux trip coil.
**L0:** Cu-Cr alloy contacts (vacuum bottle, single-source HIGH), Cu busbar (100% single-source), epoxy resin, ceramic (Al₂O₃), borosilicate glass.

### L3-3.3 Protection / NA-protection → L2 → L1 → L0

`L2 alternatives:`

| Alt. (relay platform) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Multifunction digital relay (IED, IEC 61850) | ~85% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | industry standard for new builds |
| Discrete electromechanical | ~10% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | analyst estimate |
| Programmable logic-based (SIPROTEC/REF/MICOM) | ~5% (special apps) | → Stable | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L1:** IED unit (Siemens SIPROTEC / ABB REF / Schneider MiCOM), CT/VT, NA-protection relay (DIN VDE V 0124-100), aux relays, terminal blocks, DIN-rail PSU.
**L0:** Si (MCU/FPGA), FR4 PCB, Cu, ferrite (CT cores).

### L3-3.4 Metering & SCADA gateway → L2 → L1 → L0

`single-source: true` at L2 (one panel per plant).
**L1:** Class-0.5S energy meter (MID-certified), substation-grade RTU, IEC 60870-5-104 / IEC 61850 gateway, fiber media converter.
**L0:** Si, FR4 PCB, Cu, optical glass fiber.

---

sections.title: L4-4 — Control / EMS Subsystem
sections.content:
```
L4-4 Control / EMS
 ├─ L3-4.1 EMS controller hardware
 ├─ L3-4.2 EMS software stack
 ├─ L3-4.3 Comms / network
 └─ L3-4.4 Cybersecurity layer
```

### L4-4 → L3 [Control architecture]

`single-source: true` (every BESS has these four control blocks).

### L3-4.1 EMS controller hardware → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alt. (controller class) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Industrial PC (x86, fanless) | ~55% | → Stable | MED | analyst estimate |
| PLC (Siemens S7, Beckhoff) | ~30% | → Stable | MED | analyst estimate |
| ARM SBC (NVIDIA Jetson, edge AI) | ~15% | ↑ Growing | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L1:** SBC/IPC mainboard, DIO modules, redundant PSU, SSD, mounting rail.
**L0:** Si, FR4, Cu, NAND flash, Al heatsink.

### L3-4.2 EMS software stack → L2 → L1

`L0 N/A — software has no raw material. Treat L1 = software module as terminal.`

| Alt. (EMS vendor / platform) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Tesla Autobidder/Powerhub | ~15% (global integrator) | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [WoodMac](https://www.woodmac.com/press-releases/tesla-remains-the-top-global-producer-of-battery-energy-storage-systems-in-2024-but-sungrow-narrows-the-gap/) |
| Sungrow iEnergyCloud | ~14% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [Enlit/WoodMac](https://www.enlit.world/library/sungrow-fluence-and-tesla-leading-bess-integrators-says-wood-mackenzie) |
| Fluence Mosaic / IQ | ~10–12% | → Stable | HIGH | [WoodMac](https://www.woodmac.com/press-releases/2024-press-releases/tesla-takes-sungrows-crown-as-lead-global-producer-of-bess-in-2023/) |
| Wärtsilä GEMS | ~5% | → Stable | MED | [Wärtsilä GEMS](https://www.wartsila.com/energy/energy-storage/technology/gems-digital-energy-platform) |
| Third-party DE optimizers (Entrix, Suena, Next Kraftwerke, EnAppSys) | ~10–15% (DE merchant) | ↑ Growing | LOW | analyst estimate |
| Other OEM / in-house | ~40% | mixed | LOW | analyst estimate |

`L0: software — record as N/A; treat L1 software module as terminal.`

### L3-4.3 Comms / network → L2 → L1 → L0

`L2 single-source` (managed switch + router + cellular backup is universal).
**L1:** managed Ethernet switch (IEC 61850), cellular modem (LTE-M / 5G), fiber media converter, panel PC HMI.
**L0:** Si, FR4, Cu, optical fiber (silica glass).

### L3-4.4 Cybersecurity → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alt. (firewall class) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| OT-purpose firewall (Tofino, Hirschmann EAGLE, Moxa) | ~50% | ↑ Growing | MED | [IEC 62443 industry trend](https://www.iec.ch/blog/iec-62443-cyber-security-action-plan) |
| General-purpose NGFW (Fortinet, Palo Alto) | ~40% | → Stable | MED | analyst estimate |
| Software-only segmentation (no appliance) | ~10% | ↓ Declining | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L1:** firewall appliance, IDS sensor, secure gateway.
**L0:** Si, FR4, Cu.

---

sections.title: L4-5 — Civil & Site Subsystem
sections.content:
```
L4-5 Civil & Site
 ├─ L3-5.1 Foundation
 ├─ L3-5.2 Cable trenching & ducting
 ├─ L3-5.3 Perimeter security
 └─ L3-5.4 Lightning protection / earthing
```

### L4-5 → L3

`single-source: true` (these four blocks are standard German EPC scope).

### L3-5.1 Foundation → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alt. (foundation type) | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Reinforced concrete pad (in-situ) | ~75% | → Stable | HIGH | DE EPC standard practice |
| Precast concrete plinth | ~20% | ↑ Growing | MED | analyst estimate |
| Steel skid frame on screw piles | ~5% | ↑ Emerging (fast-deploy) | LOW | analyst estimate |

**L1:** rebar mesh, concrete slab, anchor bolts.
**L0:** Portland cement (CEM II/B-S 32.5), aggregate (sand+gravel), water, steel rebar (B500B), PE moisture barrier.

| Alt. cement type | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CEM II (blended, low-CO₂) | ~60% (DE) | ↑ Growing | HIGH | [VDZ German Cement Statistics](https://www.vdz-online.de/en/) |
| CEM I (Portland) | ~30% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | [VDZ](https://www.vdz-online.de/en/) |
| CEM III (slag) | ~10% | → Stable | MED | [VDZ](https://www.vdz-online.de/en/) |

### L3-5.2 Cable trenching & ducting → L2 → L1 → L0

`single-source L2.` **L1:** HDPE conduit (DN110/160), pull pits, MV cable (NA2XS(F)2Y 12/20 kV), LV cable.
**L0:** HDPE polymer (cable jacket + conduit), Cu conductor (~95%) / Al conductor (~5%), XLPE insulation, PE/PVC bedding.

### L3-5.3 Perimeter security → L2 → L1 → L0

`single-source L2.` **L1:** mesh fence panels, posts, gate, access controller.
**L0:** Hot-dip galvanized steel (DIN EN ISO 1461) ~95%, Al ~5%, PVC coating (optional).

### L3-5.4 Lightning protection / earthing → L2 → L1 → L0

`single-source L2.` Class III LPS per IEC 62305 typical for BESS plant.
**L1:** air terminals, down conductors, earth electrodes, equipotential bonding bus.
**L0:** Cu (~70%) / hot-dip galvanized steel (~25%) / stainless 1.4571 (~5%) for buried electrodes (analyst est., MED).

---

sections.title: B.4 — Component Category Map (key cross-cutting components)
sections.content:
### Component category: **Lithium prismatic LFP cell**

| BOM position | Function | Safety-critical | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| L1 within L2 battery rack (under L3-1.1, L4-1) | Reversible Li-intercalation energy store | YES (thermal runaway risk) | NMC, Na-ion, LTO (per chemistry table) |

### Component category: **IGBT/SiC power switch**

| BOM position | Function | Safety-critical | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| L1 within L2 PCS power module (under L3-2.1, L4-2) | DC↔AC switching at 1500 Vdc / 690 Vac | YES (arc/explosion if fault) | Si IGBT, SiC MOSFET, GaN |

### Component category: **Vacuum interrupter**

| BOM position | Function | Safety-critical | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| L1 within L2 MV CB (under L3-3.2, L4-3) | Interrupt fault current at 24 kV | YES (substation safety) | SF6 puffer (legacy), vacuum (dominant), solid-insulated |

### Component category: **MCU / microcontroller**

| BOM position | Function | Safety-critical | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| L1 within L2 BMS PCBA (L3-1.2) | Cell-level voltage/temp monitoring + balancing | YES | per BMS chipset choice |
| L1 within L2 EMS controller (L3-4.1) | Optimization runtime | NO | x86 vs ARM vs PLC |
| L1 within L2 protection IED (L3-3.3) | Trip logic | YES | per IED platform |

`Insight: MCU appears at 3+ BOM positions but Core Job differs — cell safety (BMS) vs optimization (EMS) vs trip protection (IED). Same component, different competitive set per position.`

---

sections.title: B.5 — Company Position
sections.content:
```
L5  Behind-the-Meter Containerized BESS Plant (1–5 MWh)   ← MEIN SOLAR position (L5)
 │                                                            (entire BOM below = Mein Solar's deliverable)
 ├─ L4-1  Battery Storage Subsystem
 ├─ L4-2  Power Conversion Subsystem
 ├─ L4-3  MV Grid Coupling Subsystem
 ├─ L4-4  Control / EMS Subsystem
 └─ L4-5  Civil & Site Subsystem
```

**Mein Solar is the L5 EPC integrator.** It does not manufacture L4 or L3 components — it specifies, procures, integrates, certifies, and warranties the L5 deliverable. Per the BOM doc rule: "If the company's product IS the L5 output, mark the company at L5 and note that the entire BOM below represents their product's composition."

**Both BOMs (per B.5 callout):**
- **Upward context BOM:** N/A — Mein Solar's product IS the L5 end product, not a component in a larger system.
- **Downward product BOM:** the full L4→L0 decomposition above.

---

sections.title: B.6 — Strategic BOM Adjacency
sections.content:
### Horizontal BOM Adjacency (other L4 subsystems Mein Solar could supply IF they were a component supplier)

`Not applicable in the conventional sense — Mein Solar is the L5 integrator, not an L4 component supplier. Adjacency analysis here is at the L5 level: other L5 deliverables Mein Solar could integrate at C&I real-estate sites.`

| Adjacent L5 deliverable (same buyer, lessor of nonresidential building) | Capability gap | Market size (DE est.) | Strategic fit |
|---|---|---|---|
| L5 PV+BESS hybrid plant | LOW (Mein Solar's PV-EPC heritage) | €1.5–2 B/yr | STRONG |
| L5 HPC charging hub with BESS buffer | MEDIUM (BESS shared, HPC dispenser new) | €0.5–1 B/yr | STRONG |
| L5 Heat-pump + BESS + PV behind-the-meter electrification stack | MEDIUM (heat-pump trade new) | €1–2 B/yr | MEDIUM |
| L5 Tenant EV-fleet AC charging (11–22 kW) park | LOW | €0.3–0.5 B/yr | MEDIUM |
| L5 Microgrid / Inselnetz controller for tenant resilience | HIGH (islanding controls new) | €0.1–0.3 B/yr | WEAK |

### Vertical BOM Move — Up

`From L5 EPC integrator to L6 (operator / asset manager / VPP aggregator).` This is the natural upward move: from delivering the asset to operating it.

| Factor | Evaluation |
|---|---|
| Additional capabilities required | 24/7 NOC, EPEX/regelleistung trading desk, asset performance optimization |
| Capital investment | €2–5 M (NOC + software platform + trading licenses) |
| Existing customer leverage | HIGH (lessor wants single counterparty for life-cycle) |
| Competitive response | Compete with VPP aggregators (Next Kraftwerke, Entrix, Suena) and OEMs (Tesla Autobidder) |
| Time to market | 18–24 months |

### Vertical BOM Move — Down (component-level entry)

`Mein Solar moving from L5 integrator down to L4/L3 component manufacturing.` **Not strategic** — would conflict with single-source EPC promise (compete with own suppliers) and require manufacturing capex Mein Solar lacks.

| Target position | Verdict |
|---|---|
| L4-1 own container integration (CTOS-style) | REJECT — competes with Sungrow/Tesla suppliers |
| L4-4 own EMS software stack | CONSIDER — moat-building; €1–3 M dev cost; differentiates EPC offer |
| L4-5 in-house civil contracting | CONSIDER — already partially executed; vertical efficiency |

### Cross-system BOM positions (FP-extension domains)

`Mein Solar's L5-integrator capability transfers to non-storage assets.`

| Target system | BOM role | Shared capability | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|---|
| L5 Large-scale PV plant | Same EPC role | MV coupling, cert, civil 100% shared | Battery-specific knowledge unused (already incumbent) |
| L5 HPC charging hub | Same EPC role | MV coupling, cert, civil shared | HPC dispenser specification new |
| L5 Industrial microgrid | Same EPC role | EMS, MV, civil shared | Islanding controls new |
| L5 PV+Wind hybrid park | Same EPC role | MV, cert shared | Wind-turbine interface new |

---

sections.title: Quality Checks
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Every L4 decomposes to L0 | ✅ (5 of 5 L4 subsystems traced to raw materials) |
| Every level transition has alternatives table OR `single-source: true` | ✅ |
| Alternative shares sum to 100% (±1%) | ✅ |
| Packaging/containment included | ✅ (L3-1.5 container, L4-5 civil) |
| Control/interface included | ✅ (L4-4 EMS) |
| Company position marked | ✅ (Mein Solar at L5, entire BOM = its product) |
| Horizontal & vertical adjacency completed | ✅ |
| BOM vs VN test passed | ✅ (every L0 ends up IN the deliverable, not USED to make it) |
| All entities trace to CFJ (storage of grid-connected energy) | ✅ |

---
